intro: The clearest split is that Algolia uses a hybrid model and fd uses a free model. Algolia is Hosted search API with typo-tolerance, faceting, and relevance tuning for e-commerce and SaaS; fd is CI/CD for Applications tool for developers. Specializes in Fast Find. The data points Algolia at e-commerce needing sub-50ms search and documentation sites with instant search, and fd at automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Pricing: Algolia is hybrid from $0.50/1K searches (Grow); fd is free, free tier. Lock-in is medium for Algolia and low for fd, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Algolia emphasises e-commerce needing sub-50ms search and documentation sites with instant search, and fd emphasises automated build/test/deploy pipelines and team collaboration on code. Only Algolia lists an enterprise tier in the data. Honest trade-off: Algolia is not for budget, simple projects; fd is not for hobby projects, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Algolia is for e-commerce; fd is for mobile; decide based on which pricing model fits your budget.
verdict_a: Choose Algolia if a hybrid pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is budget, simple projects.
verdict_b: Choose fd if a free pricing model fits your procurement and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is hobby projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as $0.50/1K searches (Grow) for Algolia and unknown for fd. Both offer a free tier for getting started. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Algolia to fd?
faq.a: Algolia is tagged with medium lock-in (medium — proprietary api, but instantsearch ui = open-source). fd is tagged with low lock-in (oss cli find replacement). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Algolia or fd?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is fd a good alternative to Algolia?
faq.a: fd is listed as fitting mobile, which overlaps with but is not identical to Algolia's scope. fd is flagged as not for hobby projects, so confirm your context does not fall there.
